CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Test Name');
CREATE TABLE related_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, test_id INT, description VARCHAR(255), FOREIGN KEY (test_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO related_table (id, test_id, description) VALUES (1, 1, 'Related Description');
SELECT rt.description FROM related_table rt JOIN test_table tt ON rt.test_id = tt.id WHERE tt.name = 'Test Name';
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (2, 'Another Name');
CREATE TABLE complex_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value INT);
SELECT SUM(value) FROM complex_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE id = 1);
WITH cte AS (SELECT id, value FROM complex_table WHERE value > 150) SELECT AVG(value) FROM cte;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (4, 'Error Test');
